pages.raw_text:
Citizens for and against the propos-
ed franchise of the Spokane Traction
company on Washington street from
Sixth avenue to Bernard street, to
Eighth avenue, to Mantle Park. APP
peared before the special council
meeting Saturday night.


Colonel BILLY" Ridpath joined the
Washington Water Power company
opposition by protesting that he
wanted to build houses on Sixth ave-
nue. and his tenants couldn't sleep
with a noisy trolley line on the
avenue.


Frank Graves led the fight for the
Traction company and charged that
the water power company was insti-
eating the attack on the franchise.
The entire matter went over to the
meeting to be held tomorrow night


The emergency ordinance for build
ing the Olive avenue bridge was Of
fered through a resolution by Coun-
Gilman Pratt without advertising the
full 20 days. The bill provides for
$4,000 for the cost.


The mayors message was referred
to the committee of the whole.
